problem.label: The Problem
problem.subtitle: Despite being one of the most active remittance regions in the world, Sub-Saharan Africa consistently pays the highest fees, suffers the slowest transfers, and has the least financial infrastructure to support its people.

painPoints.label: Average transaction cost to Sub-Saharan Africa
painPoints.note: vs 7.2% global average and 5.4% in South Asia

painPoints.label: Annual remittances to Sub-Saharan Africa
painPoints.note: Representing 10%+ of GDP for several African nations

painPoints.label: Of adults in Sub-Saharan Africa remain unbanked
painPoints.note: The largest unbanked population of any world region

solution.label: Our Answer
solution.title: A Complete Financial Ecosystem, Built for Africa
solution.subtitle: FinPay Africa goes beyond simple transfers. We are building the financial infrastructure that connects the diaspora to their home countries — quickly, simply, and affordably.

solutionPoints.title: Instant, low-cost transfers
solutionPoints.desc: Our platform reduces transfer costs to approach the 3% G20 target, saving families real money on every transaction.

solutionPoints.title: IBAN account + bank card
solutionPoints.desc: Every user receives a certified bank card and IBAN account, granting access to the global financial system.

solutionPoints.title: Mobile-first, diaspora-ready
solutionPoints.desc: Designed for the African diaspora in Europe — simple, fast, and available wherever they are.

solutionPoints.title: Event & tontine management
solutionPoints.desc: Community financial pooling tools that reflect real African social and economic practices.

chart.title: Transaction Cost by Region
chart.caption: FinPay Africa targets the G20 5% commitment, undercut to 3%.

traction.label: Traction & Proof
traction.title: The Numbers Speak
traction.subtitle: We are not just a vision. FinPay Africa is live, growing, and delivering real value to real people across Europe and Africa today.

stats.label: Market Addressable
stats.sub: annual remittance flow to Sub-Saharan Africa

whyInvest.label: The Investment Case
whyInvest.title: 5 Reasons to Invest in FinPay Africa
whyInvest.subtitle: A compelling combination of market size, timing, technology maturity, regulatory compliance, and social mission.

reasons.title: A Proven, Massive Market
reasons.body: Over $40 billion flows into Sub-Saharan Africa each year through remittances — representing 10%+ of GDP in several nations. This is not speculative: it is a deep, recurring, growing market that has barely been touched by modern fintech.

reasons.title: First-Mover Advantage
reasons.body: Transaction costs in African remittance corridors remain nearly double the global average due to limited formal infrastructure. FinPay Africa enters this gap with a certified, modern, low-cost solution before the market consolidates.

reasons.title: Live, Certified & Compliant
reasons.body: FinPay Africa is not a concept — it is operational today. We hold the certifications and partnerships required to issue real bank cards and IBAN accounts. Every transaction is processed in full compliance with EU and international financial regulations.

reasons.title: A Scalable Technology Stack
reasons.body: Built in partnership with BrightLink Technologies, our platform is modular and API-driven. Adding new countries, new corridors, or new product lines (mobile money, microloans, insurance) requires configuration, not rebuilding.

reasons.title: Social Impact — a Strategic Asset
reasons.body: ESG-aligned investors and international development institutions are actively seeking fintech opportunities with measurable social impact. FinPay Africa delivers: financial inclusion, diaspora empowerment, and reduced remittance poverty are built into our core model.

cta.description: We are currently open to strategic investors who share our vision of transforming remittances and financial inclusion across Africa. Our pitch deck is available upon request.
